title: Origin Fairness, Politeness, Retries, Backoff, and p-limit · Type-System Trace: P Limit Boundary
summary: One global concurrency number bursts a single origin, retries synchronize, Retry-After is ignored, and p-limit is treated as a complete scheduler. Predict authorization, URL identity, runtime admission, Promise ownership, resource bounds, accessibility impact, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler ts p limit boundary increment in publisher-respecting origin scheduler. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed…
objectives: Use p-limit for bounded Promise admission while retaining separate origin eligibility, priority, cancellation, and drain state.

Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove per-origin eligibility, retry classification, Retry-After, bounded jitter, p-limit boundaries, and clock control; every Promise, stream, listener, timer, socket, file, browser context, and report sink needs one cleanup owner; then change one test, mutation, package, browser binary, container, capacity, incident, restore, rollback, or residual-risk condition and reconcile release evidence. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use p-limit for bounded Promise admission while retaining separate origin eligibility, priority, cancellation, and drain state.

For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the P Limit Boundary model · Theory P Limit Boundary, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: p-limit automatically implements robots, delays, fairness, and shutdown.
